label: Format Painter
notes: Format Painter is a modal Ribbon brush. axClick reproduces the brush cursor; AppleScript can't reproduce the modal cursor state, so this stays on AX. tabName="Home" ensures the button is in the AX tree.

label: Highlight 1
notes: Primary: appleScript sets `highlight color index` (WdColorIndex enum) on the selection. Writes <w:highlight w:val="..."/> in OOXML — same property Word's Home > Text Highlight Color > No Color button clears, so user can toggle highlight off normally. Limited to 15 named WdColorIndex values; no arbitrary RGB.

label: Highlight 2
notes: Primary: appleScript `highlight color index` (named WdColorIndex). Cleared by Word's No Color button. Fallback: axShowMenuThenClick on Bright Green swatch.

label: Highlight 3
notes: Primary: appleScript `highlight color index` (named WdColorIndex). Cleared by Word's No Color button. Fallback: axShowMenuThenClick on Blue swatch.

label: Font Color 1
notes: Primary: appleScript writes Selection.Font.Color with picker RGB. Fallback: axShowMenuThenClick on Automatic swatch.

label: Font Color 2
notes: Primary: appleScript Selection.Font.Color with picker RGB. Fallback: axShowMenuThenClick on White swatch.

label: Font Color 3
notes: Primary: appleScript Selection.Font.Color with picker RGB. Fallback: axShowMenuThenClick on Red swatch.

label: Font Family

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft Word"
  set name of font object of selection to "{{param.fontName}}"
end tell

notes: Sets the selected text's font family to the picker-chosen name. Selection-scoped (uses `font object of selection` directly — `text object of selection` silently no-ops on font name set).

label: Crop
notes: axClick on the Crop Ribbon button. REQUIRES an image to be selected first — Word's contextual Picture Format tab is what carries the Crop button, and that tab only appears when an image is the active selection. Ribbind activates the tab via tabName before searching.

label: Lock Aspect Ratio
notes: Toggles Lock Aspect Ratio. REQUIRES an image selected AND the Format Picture side pane open (right-click image → Format Picture, or use the Picture Format tab → Format Picture button) so the checkbox is in the AX tree.

label: Format Painter
notes: Brush cursor via AX-click of the Ribbon button — AppleScript can't reproduce modal Ribbon brush state.

label: Font Color 1
notes: Primary: appleScript sets font color of selected text via PowerPoint object model (~140 ms). PowerPoint AS uses 8-bit RGB (0–255), unlike Word's 16-bit. No Ribbon fallback — PPT font color picker UX through Ribbon is awkward.

label: Font Color 2
notes: Primary: appleScript font color via PPT object model with picker RGB (0–255).

label: Font Color 3
notes: Primary: appleScript font color via PPT object model with picker RGB (0–255).

label: Font Family

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set font name of font of text range of selection of document window 1 to "{{param.fontName}}"
end tell

notes: Sets the selected text's font family to the picker-chosen name. PPT uses the `font name` property (not `name`).

label: Crop
notes: axClick on the Crop Ribbon button. REQUIRES an image to be selected. PowerPoint exposes Crop on the Quick Access Toolbar when an image is the active selection — no tabName activation needed.

label: Lock Aspect Ratio
notes: Toggles Lock Aspect Ratio checkbox. REQUIRES an image selected AND the Format Picture side pane open (Picture Format tab → Format Pane, or right-click → Format Picture) so the checkbox is in the AX tree.

label: Text Box

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  make new text box at end of sl with properties {left position:{{mouse.slideX}}, top:{{mouse.slideY}}, width:320, height:80}
end tell

notes: Primary: AX-press the Insert > Text Box menu bar item — PPT enters drag-to-create mode (cursor becomes crosshair, user drags to define size). No Ribbon expansion needed. Fallback (rare — only when AX press fails): appleScript inserts a 320x80 text box at cursor slide-coords via `make new text box` (concrete subclass).

label: Oval

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  set newShape to make new shape at sl with properties {shape type:rectangle, left position:200, top:150, width:200, height:150}
  set auto shape type of newShape to 9
end tell

notes: Primary: AX-press the Insert > Shape > Oval menu bar item — drag-to-create cursor armed, no Ribbon needed. Fallbacks: appleScript creates a fixed 200x150 oval (msoShapeOval=9); axShowMenuThenClick on Ribbon if menu bar dispatch + AS both fail.

label: Rectangle

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  set newShape to make new shape at sl with properties {shape type:rectangle, left position:200, top:150, width:200, height:150}
  set auto shape type of newShape to 1
end tell

notes: Primary: AX-press the Insert > Shape > Rectangle menu bar item — drag-to-create cursor armed. Fallbacks: AS fixed 200x150 rectangle (msoShapeRectangle=1); Ribbon click.

label: Rounded Rectangle

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  set newShape to make new shape at sl with properties {shape type:rectangle, left position:200, top:150, width:200, height:150}
  set auto shape type of newShape to 5
end tell

notes: Primary: AX-press Insert > Shape > Rounded Rectangle menu bar item — drag cursor armed. Fallbacks: AS msoShapeRoundedRectangle=5; Ribbon click.

label: Left Arrow (Block)

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  set newShape to make new shape at sl with properties {shape type:rectangle, left position:200, top:150, width:200, height:150}
  set auto shape type of newShape to 34
end tell

notes: Primary: appleScript msoShapeLeftArrow=34. Fallback: Ribbon click.

label: Down Arrow (Block)

dispatchRecipes.source:
tell application "Microsoft PowerPoint"
  set sl to slide of view of document window 1
  set newShape to make new shape at sl with properties {shape type:rectangle, left position:200, top:150, width:200, height:150}
  set auto shape type of newShape to 36
end tell

notes: Primary: appleScript msoShapeDownArrow=36. Fallback: Ribbon click.

label: Hide Slide
notes: AX-press the Slide Show > Hide Slide menu bar item to toggle the selected slide's hidden state (slide will be skipped during slideshow).

label: Translate Page (toggle)
notes: Pure-backend toggle via AppleScript JS injection. Uses Chrome 138+'s on-device `Translator` + `LanguageDetector` APIs (same engine as the URL-bar translate icon). Toggle keeps state in `<html data-ribbindTranslated>` + each text node's `__ribbindOrig`. No external network at translation time, no API keys, no rate limits, works on any page (PDF / SPA / file://) regardless of the URL-bar icon visibility. Two one-time setups per Chrome profile, both surfaced in Ribbind Settings → Google Chrome: (1) `View > Developer > Allow JavaScript from Apple Events` toggle (Chrome's per-profile security gate); (2) translation model download, which Chrome requires to be triggered by a real user gesture — Ribbind installs a one-shot click listener on the active tab and prompts the user to click anywhere on the page once.

label: Paste with Format
notes: Cmd+V intercepted in Word foreground. pasteType (default / unformatted / matchFormatting / keepSourceFormat / asPicture / asHtml) chosen via per-binding picker. Word uses AppleScript paste special — instant, no dialog, no Ribbon animation. Cmd+V is hijacked while Word is frontmost; other apps still get their normal Cmd+V behavior.

label: Paste with Format
notes: Cmd+V intercepted in PowerPoint foreground. PPT picker exposes default / unformatted / matchFormatting only (PPT's AS dictionary lacks paste-type support, so other Word-only types are omitted). Unformatted dispatches via NSPasteboard clipboard swap (plain-text rewrite + AS paste + auto-restore) — instant, no dialog. Cmd+V hijack scoped to PPT frontmost.
